mission: Make urban and airport mobility reliable enough to be trusted every day by matching riders, drivers, fleets, and travel programs through one marketplace with strong pricing, dispatch, identity, and safety controls.

businessModel: Mobility marketplace take rate on completed trips
Premium, airport, and reserve ride mix
Business travel and managed mobility programs
marketForces: Riders expect low-friction, high-confidence travel across on-demand, scheduled, and airport trips
Driver supply remains sensitive to earnings quality, idle time, and trust in marketplace rules
Cities and airports increasingly shape pickup logic, operating permissions, and curb access
Business travel buyers want consumer-grade convenience with administrative controls
Autonomous partnerships raise the bar for dispatch, trust, and multimodal supply orchestration
keyPartners: Maps, telematics, and future autonomous-vehicle partners

designPrinciples: Separate rider demand, driver supply, business travel, and trust operations because their economics and workflows differ materially.
Keep dispatch, pricing, identity, and safety as shared control functions reused across all product surfaces.
Treat airport, reserve, and shared rides as operationally distinct extensions of the core marketplace, not simple feature flags.
Use business and fleet tooling to deepen high-value demand and supply without fragmenting the core app model.
Design the marketplace so future autonomous or partner-operated supply can reuse existing dispatch, trust, and reporting foundations.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own consumer rider acquisition, trip conversion, airport experience, and retained usage.
Own driver onboarding, earnings quality, fleet tooling, and supply health.
Own dispatch, pricing, ETA quality, reserve orchestration, and city-specific liquidity optimization.
Own safety tooling, incident operations, identity, fraud controls, and trust governance.
Own Uber for Business, corporate travel controls, and fleet or supplier partner programs.
Provide identity, payments, APIs, analytics, experimentation, and reliability foundations for every marketplace surface.

operatingModelNotes: The strategic difficulty is balancing local-market operations with a highly reusable global marketplace core.
Most differentiated work sits in dispatch quality, trust, earnings quality, and operational execution at constrained pickup environments such as airports.
The organization only scales cleanly if marketplace controls, identity, and analytics remain shared rather than duplicated across rider, driver, fleet, and business surfaces.
